key_insight: The paper predicts phases in chemically disordered alloys by combining graph convolutional neural networks, alchemical Monte Carlo sampling, and an information-entropy metric to efficiently map complex composition–structure landscapes.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable route for high-throughput screening of high-entropy and other disordered alloys when conventional phase-search methods are too expensive or infeasible.
method: Graph Convolutional Neural Network surrogate for energy evaluation during alchemical Monte Carlo sampling
Information entropy used as a metric for how thoroughly the phase space is explored
Bond Disproportion Vector benchmarked against SOAP as a lower-cost structural descriptor
evidence_signal: The approach is demonstrated across binary, ternary, quaternary, and quinary alloy systems, including CoNi, MoW, FeNi, TaW, CoCrNi, CrFeNi, CoCrFeNi, and Alx(CoCrFeNi)1-x.
watch_item: BDV is computationally cheaper than SOAP but has applicability limits, so descriptor choice may affect robustness across alloy families.
paper_title: Information Entropy Based Crystal Structure Prediction of Chemically Disordered Alloys via Graph Convolutional Neural Networks
